_note: IQ score estimation model. When BigQuery is unavailable, Mercury estimates IQ scores by evaluating observable criteria from IQ_CRITERIA.md against page evidence. This produces an ESTIMATED score — always label it as such.
methodology: Count observable binary criteria (present/absent) per category, compute category percentages, apply category weights to derive an estimated overall score. Only criteria observable from a crawl are counted — unobservable criteria (SEO metrics, paid search, social engagement, speed scores) are excluded and the weight is redistributed.

categories.label: Investor Relations
categories.notes: All criteria observable from crawl. Largest category.

categories.label: Technical / Optimisation
categories.notes: 12 criteria require external tools (HTML validation x3, mobile speed x3, desktop speed x3, accessibility x3). Only 14 observable from crawl.

categories.label: SEO
categories.notes: ALL criteria require external SEO tools (Ahrefs/SEMrush). Not observable from crawl. Excluded from estimation — weight redistributed.

categories.label: Paid Search
categories.notes: ALL criteria require external tools. Not observable from crawl. Excluded from estimation — weight redistributed.

categories.label: Social Media
categories.notes: 18 criteria observable from crawl (presence of social links, search, RSS, share buttons, embedded feeds). 24 require external data (follower counts, engagement, ad spend).

scoring_rules.step_1: For each category with observable_criteria > 0, count how many criteria are present (1) vs absent (0) based on page evidence.
scoring_rules.step_2: Compute category_score = (criteria_present / observable_criteria) * 100 for each category.
scoring_rules.step_3: Compute weighted_overall = sum of (category_score * weight) for all categories where weight > 0.
scoring_rules.step_4: Compare weighted_overall to ftse250_reference to determine estimated band and percentile range.
scoring_rules.step_5: Report as ESTIMATED IQ score with confidence interval based on unobservable criteria coverage.

high.description: Most criteria assessed. Estimate likely within ±3 points of actual IQ score.
high.label: Estimated IQ (high confidence)

medium.description: Majority of criteria assessed. Estimate likely within ±5 points.
medium.label: Estimated IQ (medium confidence)

low.description: Significant criteria unassessed. Estimate should be treated as indicative band only.
low.label: Estimated IQ band (indicative)

positioning_bands.description: Above P75. Among the strongest sites in the FTSE 250.
Between median and P75. Solid but with clear improvement opportunities.
Near the FTSE 250 median. Typical corporate site with standard coverage.
Below median. Noticeable gaps in content coverage or section depth.
Below P25. Significant gaps across multiple categories.

reporting_rules: ALWAYS label estimated scores with [INFERENCE — estimated from observable criteria]
NEVER present an estimated score as equivalent to an official Connect.IQ score
State how many of the 321 criteria were assessable and how many were actually assessed
State which categories were excluded (SEO, Paid Search) and why
Compare to FTSE 250 median (38.6%) and show the positioning band
If company IS in the 747-company dataset, note that actual scores are available via BigQuery
Round estimated scores to one decimal place
